channel.title: PlayStation Access
channel.itunes:author: PlayStation UK
channel.itunes:subtitle: The official PlayStation UK podcast
channel.itunes:summary:
We are PlayStation Access. Celebrating everything from the world of PlayStation, our podcast is dedicated to the joy of videogames; whether that's in detailed breakdowns of the latest releases or having a laugh with our brilliant community. Expect news and nonsense in equal measure.


PlayStation Access is the official YouTube channel of PlayStation UK - a vibrant, welcoming community celebrating all things PlayStation. Join us for weekly livestreams, list features and in-depth coverage on all your favourite games.


Use the hashtag #PodSquad on social media to send in your comments!

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: All About Cosplay, with Opal Ink Cosplay
item.itunes:title: All About Cosplay, with Opal Ink Cosplay
item.itunes:subtitle: "like wearing a t-shirt of your favourite thing times a thousand"
item.description:
On the latest PlayStation Access Podcast, we are delighted to be joined by special guest Opal Ink Cosplay! Abi joins us on the Access sofa to share her experiences of cosplay - from costume construction, attending conventions and judging competitions - she has tonnes of cool stories and tips to share. She even brings in some of her brilliantly crafted cosplay items for us to take a look at!


Abi has cosplayed as some of the most well known characters in games - Ellie (The Last of Us), Atreus (God of War), Aloy (Horizon) and many more. Join us as she talks through her favourite cosplays, her most challenging builds, and discusses how incredible the cosplay community is.


PlayStation Access is the official YouTube channel of PlayStation UK - a vibrant, welcoming community celebrating all things PlayStation. Join us for gaming livestreams, list features and in-depth coverage on all your favourite games.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: Brilliant Videogame Sequels That Somehow Surpassed Their Predecessors
item.itunes:title: Brilliant Videogame Sequels That Somehow Surpassed Their Predecessors
item.itunes:subtitle: Taking brilliance to a whole new level
item.description:
With Resident Evil Requiem around the corner, the Access gang are chatting about brilliant sequels that somehow surpassed their incredible predecessors.


Join us as we share our favourite sequels, and discuss what we think makes a great sequel. What are your favourite videogame sequels? Let us know in the comments!


PlayStation Access is the official YouTube channel of PlayStation UK - a vibrant, welcoming community celebrating all things PlayStation. Join us for gaming livestreams, list features and in-depth coverage on all your favourite games.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: Romeo Is A Dead Man is the most unique game of the year (with Special Guest Suda51!)
item.itunes:title: Romeo Is A Dead Man is the most unique game of the year (with Special Guest Suda51!)
item.itunes:subtitle: The legendary game designer joins us on the sofa
item.description:
On the latest PlayStation Access Podcast, we are thrilled to be joined by the legendary Goichi Suda!


Chatting all things Romeo Is A Dead Man, the unique design philosophies behind the title, and the games that inspire Suda51.


We also announce the official name of our gaming club, share our progress on Hollow Knight, and reveal the title for February!

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: Cosy Isn't a Genre, It's a State of Mind - The PlayStation Access Podcast
item.itunes:title: Cosy Isn't a Genre, It's a State of Mind - The PlayStation Access Podcast
item.itunes:subtitle: What are your cosiest comfort games?
item.description:
On the latest episode of the PlayStation Access Podcast, the team are grabbing a cup of tea, and cosying up with their favourite comfort games. From Skyrim's calming waters, to Stardew Valley's virtual gardening, and playing Phasmophobia with friends across seas, the team have every corner covered.


Rob, Conner, and Ash also catch up about their Hollow Knight progress, and what their favourite things about it are so far. They open up the floor to you, the listeners (and watchers) to decide on the final name of the gaming book club. And of course, they read through some of the wonderful emails we've already received to our inbox.


Please do get in touch at psaccessinbox@gmail.com with your fun gaming tales, or maybe send us some more hints for Hollow Knight. We'll read out some of your emails on the next pod!

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: What Is Your 2026 Gaming Resolution?
item.itunes:title: What Is Your 2026 Gaming Resolution?
item.itunes:subtitle: New year, new platinums
item.description:
On the latest PlayStation Access Podcast, the team are ushering in the new year by sharing their gaming resolutions for 2026. From platinum targets to trying new games, the team are setting themselves some big goals for the year.


They also discuss 2026 titles they cannot wait for, compare themselves to Lara Croft (a foolhardy endeavour) and announce some cool NEW things: our 'gaming book club' (name tbc) and our PlayStation Access inbox for you to email us with your gaming stories!


Please do get in touch at playstationaccessinbox@gmail.com with your fun gaming tales, or maybe send us some hints for this month's gaming book club title OR tell us about your gaming resolutions for 2026! We'll read out some of your emails on the next pod!


PlayStation Access is the official YouTube channel of PlayStation UK - a vibrant, welcoming community celebrating all things PlayStation. Join us for weekly livestreams, list features and in-depth coverage on all your favourite games.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: 2026 Games We Can't Wait To Play (and Goodbye Rosie!)
item.itunes:title: 2026 Games We Can't Wait To Play (and Goodbye Rosie!)
item.itunes:subtitle: Backlogs beware, it's going to be quite the year
item.description:
2026 is going to be a brilliant year on PS5 with many incredible titles on the way. Join us as we share some of the games we can't wait to get our hands on.


We also say goodbye to Rosie in her final appearance as a member of the PlayStation Access team, and reminisce about some of her best moments on the channel. We'll miss you, Rosie!

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.description:

What was your personal Game of the Year? Let us know in the comments!

item.title: Videogame Acting, Celebrity Impressions and Unique Roles with Alex Jordan
item.itunes:title: Videogame Acting, Celebrity Impressions and Unique Roles with Alex Jordan
item.itunes:subtitle: It's just the one Jan Dolski actually
item.description:
On the latest PlayStation Access Podcast, we are thrilled to be joined by special guest Alex Jordan to chat all things videogame acting. From how the role of the actor has expanded, to childhood dreams leading him to acting, and some of the more unique roles of his career.


We also enjoy a celebrity impressions quiz (featuring Alex's incredible impressions) that you simply cannot miss.


PlayStation Access is the official YouTube channel of PlayStation UK - a vibrant, welcoming community celebrating all things PlayStation. Join us for weekly livestreams, list features and in-depth coverage on all your favourite games.

item.title: Can We Pick Just THREE Essential PS5 Games?
item.itunes:title: Can We Pick Just THREE Essential PS5 Games?
item.itunes:subtitle: Celebrating five years of PS5 with an incredibly difficult task
item.description:
We can't believe it's been five years since the PS5 arrived, and what a journey it's been. Incredible games have come to the groundbreaking console - so we're compiling a list of the essential titles that every PS5 owner should have in their collection.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: What Would You Banish From Games?
item.itunes:title: What Would You Banish From Games?
item.itunes:subtitle: These things have GOT. TO. GO.
item.description:
On the latest PlayStation Access Podcast, the team are discussing things in games that they feel should be banned. From luck-based rewards to sneaky hidden collectibles and overencumbered player characters - erasing these things would just make gaming life so much easier for everyone. Join us as we become judge, jury and executioner!

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: Confessing Our Most Shameful Backlog Games
item.itunes:title: Confessing Our Most Shameful Backlog Games
item.itunes:subtitle: For shame. FOR SHAME
item.description:
We all have backlogs (some are clogged and massive) - but what's the one game you're ashamed to admit you've never played?


On this episode of the PlayStation Access Podcast, the team are confessing the most shocking games in their backlogs - from surprising titles that have never been touched to beloved experiences that are yet to be finished - prepare to be shocked and appalled. For shame, you guys. FOR SHAME.

item.itunes:subtitle: The game has changed.

On the latest PlayStation Access Podcast, we're thrilled to be joined on the sofa by Samantha Béart to discuss all things acting in games. They share insight to how the process has evolved, how attitudes have changed and beyond. We also get teasers about some very exciting upcoming PS5 titles!

item.title: Incredible Games That Came Out of Nowhere
item.itunes:title: Incredible Games That Came Out of Nowhere
item.itunes:subtitle: A surprise to be sure...but a welcome one
item.description:
With Fall Guys turning 5 years old, the team are sitting down to discuss special games that came out of nowhere and blew us away with their brilliance. From sharing memories of chaotic multiplayer platforming in Fall Guys to tales of superhero quests and unique RPGs, join the team as they chat about the games that completely surprised them.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.itunes:title: What Is Your Game of the Year So Far?
item.itunes:subtitle: You've spoilt us, 2025
item.description:
We're already halfway through 2025 and what a year it's been for PS5 games. In today's episode we're each picking our Game of the Year 2025 (so far) as well as sharing your community picks. From Expedition 33 to Death Stranding 2 the standard is intimidatingly high, so which games come out on top? Let's find out!

item.title: Why Death Stranding 2 Is Kojima's Best Game in 20 Years
item.itunes:title: Why Death Stranding 2 Is Kojima's Best Game in 20 Years
item.itunes:subtitle: A spoiler-free chat all about Kojima's latest masterpiece
item.description:
On the latest PlayStation Access Podcast, the team are chatting all things Death Stranding 2: On the Beach!


Join Rob and Conner for a spoiler-free discussion that covers combat, traversal, graphics and much more - there is a lot to talk about! It's safe to say, the game has had a profound effect on Rob...


Are you playing Death Stranding 2 over the weekend? Please keep it spoiler-free, but let us know what your experience with the game has been like so far.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: New Game Announcements We MUST Talk About
item.itunes:title: New Game Announcements We MUST Talk About
item.itunes:subtitle: featuring excited noises. lots of them.
item.description:
The recent State of Play and Summer Game Fest events featured tonnes of incredible announcements - and we want to talk about them! From the unveiling of a brand new Resident Evil title, to the reveal of more Persona goodness and sequels to beloved Soulslike games - join us as we discuss it all and make lots of excited noises along the way.


What was the one announcement that got you most excited? Use #podsquad across Bluesky and X - let us know!

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.itunes:title: Our Favourite Soulslike Multiplayer Memories
item.itunes:subtitle: Plus a quiz all about Souls bosses!
item.description:
With the release of Elden Ring Nightreign, the PlayStation Access team are sharing their favourite Soulslike multiplayer memories - including missing NPCs, jolly cooperation and thwarting invaders.


They also drill down into what makes the shared experiences of these games so special, and a boss-sized quiz sees Rosie and Alex square off once more.

They also discuss what they think makes a good trophy list and the team chat about why they love going after platinums, before Rosie and Alex go head-to-head in a perilous platinum quiz.

item.title: The best games we've played so far in 2025 - Atomfall, Doom: The Dark Ages, and more!
item.itunes:title: The best games we've played so far in 2025 - Atomfall, Doom: The Dark Ages, and more!
item.itunes:subtitle: 2025 has served up some crackers already - and more are around the corner!
item.description:
On the latest PlayStation Access Podcast, we welcome Rosie back to the fold and she's been playing loads of games during her time off - that she can't wait to tell us about. Staff Writer Conner makes his PlayStation Access Podcast debut and shares all from his hands-on with Doom: The Dark Ages.


Rob talks about all the fun he's been having with Hitman World of Assassination on PS VR2, and Dave briefly mentions his futile attempts to beat the first boss in The First Berserker: Khazan before moving on to discussing how much he's been enjoying Atomfall!


What new games have you been enjoying?

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.
item.itunes:summary:
On the latest PlayStation Access Podcast, we welcome Rosie back to the fold and she's been playing loads of games during her time off - that she can't wait to tell us about. Staff Writer Conner makes his PlayStation Access Podcast debut and shares all from his hands-on with Doom: The Dark Ages.


Rob talks about all the fun he's been having with Hitman World of Assassination on PS VR2, and Dave briefly mentions his futile attempts to beat the first boss in The First Berserker: Khazan before moving on to discussing how much he's been enjoying Atomfall!


What new games have you been enjoying?

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: It's God of War's 20th Anniversary and we are going to talk about it
item.itunes:title: It's God of War's 20th Anniversary and we are going to talk about it
item.itunes:subtitle: Pod of War
item.description:
Join Rob, Nath and Dave as they celebrate Kratos and the entire God of War pantheon which turns an incredible twenty years old TODAY! We'll be talking about our memories of each of the games, and try to express what God of War has meant to gamers worldwide. Happy birthday Kratos!


PlayStation Access is the official YouTube channel of PlayStation UK - a vibrant, welcoming community celebrating all things PlayStation. Join us for weekly livestreams, list features and in-depth coverage on all your favourite games.

item.itunes:title: The Best Co-op Games on PS5 - And Why Split Fiction Could Be GOTY
item.itunes:subtitle: Extremely Jolly Co-operation
item.description:
The team have been playing Split Fiction, the newly released co-op adventure from the creators of It Takes Two and A Way Out. Not only is it already a highlight of the year - packed with inventive, delightful ways of playing together - but it's sparked a discussion about the joys of co-op gaming in general, and our favourite PlayStation co-op titles in particular.


What are our top co-op games of all time? Join Rob, Dave, and Nath to find out...

item.title: Incredible Games Coming To PS5, Astro Bot Speedrun Rivalries, and A Nail-Biting Quiz
item.itunes:title: Incredible Games Coming To PS5, Astro Bot Speedrun Rivalries, and A Nail-Biting Quiz
item.description:
With all the incredible reveals at the latest PlayStation State of Play, the Access gang couldn't wait to get on the sofa and talk all about the fantastic games that are coming to PS5.


From Ghost of Yōtei, to Hell Is Us, Dragon Age: The Veilguard and Legacy of Kain Soul Reaver 1 and 2 Remastered, there is much to discuss. The announcement of imminent Astro Bot DLC in the form of speedrun levels has the team feeling competitive - and a nail-biting quiz has controversial results.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: The Magic Of Adventure Games, With Charles Cecil
item.itunes:title: The Magic Of Adventure Games, With Charles Cecil
item.description:
On the latest PlayStation Access Podcast, we are delighted to be joined by Charles Cecil, Founder and CEO of Revolution Software. Charles tells us all about the return of Broken Sword, the challenges the studio faced in bringing classic PS1 title Broken Sword: The Shadow of the Templars to PS5, and more. Originally released in 1996, the new version of this charming point-and-click adventure boasts incredible 4K visuals and remastered audio.


Broken Sword - Shadow of the Templars: Reforged is out now on PS5, and director Charles Cecil shares fascinating stories about working on adventure games, meeting fans and the thinking behind that infamous goat puzzle. That goat still haunts some of us...

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: Creating Our Dream Gaming Music Festival
item.itunes:title: Creating Our Dream Gaming Music Festival
item.description:
With festival season in full swing, the Access gang thought it would be fun to create our own dream gaming music festival! Featuring our favourite game soundtracks, performances from talented composers and even appearances from videogame characters. We also decide what food should be available at the event, which characters would be the worst to share a tent with, and who would be a nightmare to crowdsurf.


What videogame soundtracks, fictional characters and real-life musicians would you want to see headline the festival? And which characters do you think would make the best campmates (or maybe, the worst?) Use #podsquad and let us know across socials!

item.title: Desert Island Games Competition
item.itunes:title: Desert Island Games Competition
item.description:
On the latest PlayStation Access Podcast, the team have been set a tricky challenge - to each pick 6 games to bring with them to PlayStation Paradise Island.


They must pick a game for each of the following categories:

- An epic time sink to help fill the days (so, a game that lasts a long time)

- Multiplayer game you can beat everyone at (for the the regular beach meet-up)

- A game to play when you are feeling homesick

- A game to help get your anger out when a storm destroys your hut

- A game with great AI companions (for when you're felling lonely)

- A game that features a desert island or coastal region


But that's not all! We're making it competitive and letting YOU decide who had the best picks. Keep your eyes on the YouTube channel community tab in the coming days (https://www.youtube.com/@PlayStationAccess/community) for the poll to choose our winner!


What six games would you choose? Let us know in the comments!

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: What Makes The Ultimate Survival Horror Game?
item.itunes:title: What Makes The Ultimate Survival Horror Game?
item.description:
On the latest PlayStation Access Podcast, we are thrilled to be joined by Adam Vian from SFB Games to talk about their terrifying survival horror title Crow Country, coming to PS4 & PS5 on 9 May.


From discussing the influences behind their latest game, reliving horrifying theme park ride experiences to delving into what makes sthe ultimate survival horror experience, this week's PlayStation Access Podcast is a must listen for fans of horror.


Adam also talks about his incredible collection of older PlayStation titles and highlights some of his favourites. These are some seriously lesser known games - some of them even Rosie hasn't heard of before. Oh, and we also pit Rosie against Adam in a tricky survival horror quiz!


What is your favourite survival horror title? Do you have any hidden gems from the genre that you would recommend? Let us know in the comments!

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: From Acting In Games To Creating Them, With Abubakar Salim!
item.itunes:title: From Acting In Games To Creating Them, With Abubakar Salim!
item.description:
On the latest PlayStation Access Podcast, we are delighted to be joined by BAFTA nominated actor and founder & CEO of Surgent Studios Abubakar Salim. From acting in games to making them - our special guest starred in Assassin's Creed Origins and now is Game Director for their first game, Tales of Kenzera: Zau.


Join us for a fascinating discussion about game development, announcing their studio's first ever game to the world, and what makes Tales of Kenzera: Zau such a special title.


Abubakar also shares the games that inspired his younger self, chats about his career in games and the character of Bayek, and we test Dave with a particularly tricky quiz.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth Spoilercast
item.itunes:title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth Spoilercast
item.description:
*SPOILER WARNING* Do not listen to this unless you've finished Final Fantasy VII Rebirth!


Rob and Rosie are joined on this very special podcast by Amy, huge Final Fantasy fan, to chat all things FFVII Rebirth.


They've each finished the story of Rebirth and can't wait any longer to talk about that ending! What does the ending mean for our beloved characters and for the future of the series? If you too have reached the game's epic conclusion, join us for some spoilerific chat!

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: Incredible Game Worlds, Dragon's Dogma 2 Pawns, and Cinema Gaming Sessions
item.itunes:title: Incredible Game Worlds, Dragon's Dogma 2 Pawns, and Cinema Gaming Sessions
item.description:
On the latest PlayStation Access Podcast, the team are discussing those magical game worlds that transported them to another time and place. From the beautiful open worlds of Red Dead Redemption and Final Fantasy VII Rebirth, to the incredibly imaginative realms of Bioshock Infinite and Dishonored - games have such a powerful way of sweeping us off to some truly remarkable places.


The gang also chat about how they've met friends through games, Rosie shares her plans to create characters from her boyfriend list in Tekken 8's character creator, and hiring an entire cinema screen to play games. We should definitely do that at some point.


What are the game worlds that mean the most to you? Use #podsquad and let us know in the comments or across socials!

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.description:

We also find time for Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth and Rosie's confusing Lord of the Rings recap. While it's sad to say farewell to Ash, the podcast will continue, so see you again in two weeks!


Do you have comments and questions for the gang? Use #podsquad across socials to send them in!

With so many brilliant games on PlayStation, we take a moment to pick out some underappreciated favourites that absolutely deserve your time! Featuring plenty of indie game recommendations, a quiz that isn't as crystal clear as it seems on paper, and the lovely CozyGamerKat as a special guest on the Access sofa. Don't forget to use #PodSquad to send in your comments and questions for future pods!

item.itunes:title: Videogame Demos, Final Fantasy 16, and Zangief's Pecs
item.description:
This week, we're talking all things demos and behind-the-scenes previews. Just how does that pesky gaming sausage get made? Rosie shares her experiences with the Final Fantasy XVI demo, the origin of Rob’s passion for water graphics is revealed, and some particularly cursed singing highlights Zangief's greatest assets. Don't forget to use #PodSquad to send in your comments and questions for future pods!

item.title: Destiny 2: Lightfall Special (feat. James Tsai Interview)
item.itunes:title: Destiny 2: Lightfall Special (feat. James Tsai Interview)
item.description:
Destiny 2: Lightfall is coming to PS4 and PS5 on 28th February, so we're celebrating with a very special episode of the PlayStation Access Podcast. Join Rob and Nath, alongside Destiny 2 experts Zoe (@zoe_ttv) and Byf (@MyNameIsByf), as they dive into all the goodies Destiny 2 offers every kind of player, and stay tuned for an interview with Bungie Creative Director James Tsai later in the episode. Don't forget to leave your comment with #PodSquad for the chance to be read out on future pods!

item.title: Batman in Gaming, Gotham Knights, and Riddler Riddles
item.itunes:title: Batman in Gaming, Gotham Knights, and Riddler Riddles
item.description:
The PlayStation Access podcast is back and better than ever! We're kicking things off with a look at the influence of Batman on gaming, a talk about the upcoming Gotham Knights - complete with an interview with the game's Creative Director Patrick Redding, and Executive Producer Fleur Marty - and a far too detailed breakdown of what villains we'd personally create to infiltrate the Batman series. Don't forget to use #PodSquad to send in your comments and questions!
